user_query:
I’d like a small local, event-triggered automation for my personal workspace that activates whenever a new flight roster CSV is dropped into input/inbox. Please implement it so that any roster CSV in input/inbox that hasn’t been processed yet is treated as a new event, and only those new files are processed. Use the following inputs:

- Flight roster CSV (event trigger): input/inbox/roster_2024_11.csv
- Exhibitions HTML (local web content to parse): data/exhibits.html
- Preferences: config/preferences.json
- Favorite artists: notes/favorite_artists.txt
- Inspiration notes: notes/inspiration.md

Behavior and matching logic:
1) Parse the roster CSV to find OFF days (duty == "OFF", case-insensitive). Consider events whose dates fall within the inclusive min..max date range present in the roster file.
2) From data/exhibits.html, extract structured event records with these fields: title (from .title), date (from the parent .event element’s data-date attribute in YYYY-MM-DD), city (from .city), tags (all <li> tags inside .tags). Only keep events whose tags intersect preferences.interest_tags in config/preferences.json.
3) Match events whose event date is an OFF day from the roster. For each matched event, compute:
   - event_title
   - event_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - city
   - tags (array)
   - matched_off_day (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - interest_match (boolean: true if any tag intersects preferences.interest_tags)
   - favorite_artist_match (boolean: true if any name from notes/favorite_artists.txt appears in the event title or description text, case-insensitive)
   - inspiration_quote (the first blockquote line in notes/inspiration.md that contains the word "flight", case-insensitive; omit this field if none found)
   - source_file_hashes: an object with sha256 hashes for the five inputs above under keys roster_csv, exhibits_html, preferences_json, favorite_artists_txt, inspiration_md
4) Idempotency/state: Maintain output/state/processed_manifest.json with an array of processed roster files, each entry containing file_path, sha256, and processed_at (ISO datetime). On each run, process only roster CSVs in input/inbox whose sha256 is not already listed.
5) Dedupe by title if config/preferences.json has dedupe_by_title == true (case-insensitive).

Deliverables (write all outputs locally):
- For each newly processed roster file, write a JSON file at output/matches/matches_<roster_basename>.json containing an array of the matched event objects with the fields above.
- Also write a summary CSV at output/reports/summary_<roster_basename>.csv with columns: off_day, matched_events_count, favorite_artist_flagged_count (count where favorite_artist_match == true) for each OFF day in the roster date range.
- Update output/state/processed_manifest.json as specified.

Assumptions/validation:
- Treat any file placed in input/inbox as potentially new; use the manifest to decide if it’s already processed (by sha256, not just name).
- All parsing should be robust to whitespace/case. Do not use network calls; all inputs are local.
- The task is complete when the above outputs are created for roster_2024_11.csv and the manifest reflects it.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
